title: centaurea parviflora
intro: Centaurea parviflora, also known as the small-flowered knapweed, is a flowering plant native to Europe and Asia. It is a member of the daisy family and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Centaurea parviflora can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various forms.
